MANGODESK HELP PANELS — ALL TABS + OVERVIEW HOW TO ADD TO YOUR FILE: 1. Open index.html in Notepad 2. Press Ctrl+End to go to the very bottom 3. Open this file in Notepad, press Ctrl+A then Ctrl+C 4. Click back into index.html and press Ctrl+V 5. Press Ctrl+S to save PLACING EACH PANEL: Each panel has a comment above it saying which tab it belongs to. Search your file for that tab section and paste the matching panel at the very top inside that section. The OVERVIEW panel goes above your tab bar at the top of the page. HELP BUTTON — add this anywhere in your nav: ============================================================ -->

🤖 Ask Claude
MangoDesk — How this dashboard works — click to expand
▼

MangoDesk is a personal financial planning and estimating tool. It gives you a live picture of your financial position — income, expenses, property performance, debt, and net worth — all updating automatically as you enter data.

⚠ MangoDesk is not an accounting package. All figures are estimates and budgets for planning purposes. Enter your best estimates and update them as real figures come in — statements, actual bills, new valuations. The goal is a clear financial planning picture, not exact bookkeeping.

Properties

Add each property with its value, mortgage, rental income and running costs. Equity and rental profit or loss flow to Dashboard and Cashflow automatically.

feeds dashboard + cashflow

Vehicles

Add vehicles with their value and finance owing. Equity flows to net worth. Repayments flow to Cashflow automatically — no double entry needed.

feeds dashboard + cashflow

Insurance

A policy tracker only. Store insurance details and renewal dates so you get Dashboard alerts before anything lapses. Does not connect to other tabs.

standalone reminders

Superannuation

A retirement planning tool. Budget how you will spend your super in retirement or estimate what you will have if still working. Balance feeds into net worth.

feeds dashboard

Shares & Other

Track your share portfolio, crypto, managed funds and other investments. Total value feeds into your net worth on the Dashboard.

feeds dashboard

Cashflow

Your annual financial picture. Pulls property income and losses plus vehicle repayments automatically. Add wages and other income manually. Shows net profit or loss for the year.

receives from properties + vehicles

Bills

A bill reminder hub only. Enter bills so they alert you on the Dashboard when due. Not connected to Cashflow or net worth — purely reminders.

standalone reminders

Creditors

Track credit cards and debts. The total balance is deducted from your net worth so your financial position reflects what you actually owe.

feeds dashboard

52 Week Planner

A weekly budget tool. Enter actual bills and payments week by week and change figures as you go. Great for tracking real spending against your annual estimates.

standalone budget tool

Loan Calculator

A standalone tool to calculate repayments before committing to any loan or mortgage. Does not feed anywhere — just use it to run numbers and compare scenarios.

standalone tool

Notepad

A private scratchpad for financial notes, goals and reminders. Saves automatically. Nothing connects anywhere — just a handy place to jot things down.

standalone

Keeping it current

Update figures whenever real ones come in — valuations, mortgage statements, super statements, actual bills. Every update flows through to your Dashboard instantly.

Dashboard — click to expand setup instructions
▼

Your financial home screen. Everything here updates automatically as you add and change data in other tabs — no manual updating needed on this page.

⚠ All figures shown are estimates based on what you have entered across all tabs. Update them regularly as real figures come in to keep your picture accurate.

Your name

Enter your name in the Dashboard Owner field. It appears in the topbar and on any reports you export.

Net worth summary

Shows your total estimated financial position — property equity, super, shares and vehicles, minus mortgage debt, creditors and bills outstanding. Updates instantly when any tab changes.

auto-updates from all tabs

Alerts & reminders

Shows upcoming bills and insurance renewals automatically. Add your own custom reminders for anything you want flagged on the home screen.

auto-updates from bills + insurance

Saving your data

Use Save Data regularly to back up your information. Use Load Data to restore a backup. Do this after any significant changes so you never lose your work.

Export report

Export a summary of your financial position at any time — useful for sharing with a financial adviser or keeping a snapshot of where you stood.

Currency

Change currency from the selector at the top. If you change it, check that all figures still display correctly across tabs as this may affect formatting.

Properties — click to expand setup instructions
▼

Track all your real estate — family home, investment properties, holiday houses. Add each property once and MangoDesk automatically updates your net worth and cashflow.

⚠ All property values, rental income and expenses are estimates for planning purposes. MangoDesk is not an accounting package — use it to budget and get a picture of your financial position.

Adding a property

Click + Add Property. Enter the name, estimated current value, mortgage balance, property type and lender. Add as many properties as you own — there is no limit.

Equity is automatic

MangoDesk calculates your equity (value minus mortgage) automatically. Every change instantly updates your net worth on the Dashboard.

feeds dashboard

Rental income & expenses

Enter estimated rental income and running costs (rates, water, maintenance, management fees) here. They automatically appear in Cashflow — no double entry needed.

feeds cashflow

Profit or loss

If rent exceeds costs it shows as profit in Cashflow. If costs are higher it shows as a loss. This tells you whether each property is performing financially.

feeds cashflow

Keeping figures current

Update the property value when you get a valuation and the mortgage balance from your latest statement. Dashboard net worth updates automatically each time.

About the figures

Expenses like water at $480/quarter are budget estimates — actual bills may differ. This is intentional. MangoDesk is a planning tool, not a bookkeeping system.

Data that flows from Properties to other tabs

Rental income→Appears as income in Cashflow automatically
Property expenses→Appears as expenses in Cashflow automatically
Equity→Updates Net Worth on Dashboard instantly

Vehicles — click to expand setup instructions
▼

Track every vehicle you own — cars, motorbikes, boats, caravans — and any finance still owing. Vehicle equity feeds directly into your net worth.

⚠ Vehicle values and repayments are estimates. Update the value periodically as vehicles depreciate — this directly affects your net worth figure.

Adding a vehicle

Click + Add Vehicle. Enter the vehicle name, estimated current market value, finance owing (enter 0 if none), lender and monthly repayment. Add as many as you own.

Equity is automatic

Vehicle equity (value minus finance owing) is calculated automatically and added to your Dashboard net worth. Update values as the vehicle depreciates.

feeds dashboard

Repayments flow to Cashflow

Vehicle repayments automatically appear as an expense in Cashflow. You do not need to enter them there — they pull through from here automatically.

feeds cashflow

Keeping figures current

Update the vehicle value periodically — cars lose value over time. Update the finance balance from your latest loan statement to keep equity accurate.

Data that flows from Vehicles to other tabs

Repayments→Appear as expenses in Cashflow automatically
Equity→Updates Net Worth on Dashboard instantly
ASSET PORTFOLIO
Vehicles & Trailers
🚗Vehicles & Trailers View: Monthly
Show amounts:
Insurance — click to expand setup instructions
▼

A policy tracker — keep all your insurance details in one place so nothing lapses without you knowing. Insurance does not connect to other tabs or affect your net worth.

⚠ Insurance is a standalone tracker only. Premiums entered here do not flow to Cashflow or affect your net worth. It is purely for keeping policy details and renewal reminders in one place.

Adding a policy

Click + Add Policy. Enter the policy name, provider, policy number, annual premium and renewal date. Add all your policies — home, contents, car, health, life, income protection.

Renewal alerts

Policies with a renewal date within 30 days automatically appear as alerts on your Dashboard. You will always know what is coming up before it lapses.

feeds dashboard alerts

Updating policies

When you renew a policy, update the renewal date and premium. This keeps your alerts accurate and gives you an up to date record of all your cover.

What to include

Add every policy you hold — home and contents, car insurance, health insurance, life insurance, income protection, landlord insurance for investment properties.

ASSET PROTECTION
Insurance Policies
POLICIES
—
🛡Insurance Policies

Superannuation — click to expand setup instructions
▼

A retirement planning tool. Use it to budget how you intend to spend your super in retirement and see estimated future balances — or use it to estimate what you will have if you are still working. Set it up with your best estimates then update as things change.

⚠ Super figures are estimates and projections only. Update your balance when you receive a statement — usually quarterly. MangoDesk is not a financial planning tool and this is not financial advice.

Adding your fund

Enter your fund name, current balance and member number. If you have multiple funds add each one separately. The total feeds into your Dashboard net worth.

feeds dashboard

Retirement projections

Set your expected retirement age, planned annual drawdown, and inflation rate. MangoDesk projects your estimated balance year by year so you can see if your super will last.

Extra deposits & withdrawals

Add extra contribution lines (salary sacrifice, voluntary contributions) and lump sum withdrawals. Each line adjusts the projected balance so you can model different scenarios.

Inflation setting

Set an inflation rate to see how the real value of your super changes over time. Adjust this to model optimistic or conservative scenarios for your retirement planning.

Currency

You can change the currency display. If you do, check that all super figures still display correctly as currency changes may affect number formatting across the tab.

Keeping it current

Update your balance each time you receive a statement. Adjust your planned withdrawals and contributions as your life circumstances change. The projections update instantly.

Data that flows from Super to other tabs

Super balance→Updates Net Worth on Dashboard instantly
RETIREMENT PLANNING
Superannuation
SUPER BALANCE
—
🎯Superannuation — Retirement Planner
% p.a.

Shares & Other Assets — click to expand setup instructions
▼

Track your share portfolio, crypto, managed funds, term deposits and any other investments. The total value feeds into your Dashboard net worth automatically.

⚠ Share and investment values change constantly. The figures here are what you manually enter — MangoDesk does not connect to live market data. Update regularly to keep your net worth accurate.

Adding an asset

Click + Add Asset. Enter the asset name, broker or platform, current estimated value, and cost base (what you originally paid for it). Add each holding separately.

Capital gain tracking

The difference between current value and cost base shows your estimated capital gain or loss. This is for planning awareness only — not a tax calculation.

What to include

Add shares, ETFs, managed funds, crypto, term deposits, or any other investment asset. Anything with a value that contributes to your overall financial position.

Keeping figures current

Update values regularly — especially shares which change daily. Even a rough monthly update keeps your net worth picture reasonably accurate.

feeds dashboard

Data that flows from Shares to other tabs

Total portfolio value→Updates Net Worth on Dashboard instantly

Cashflow — click to expand setup instructions
▼

Your annual financial picture. See all income and expenses in one place — what is coming in, what is going out, and whether you will end the year in profit or loss. Property income and losses plus vehicle repayments pull in automatically.

⚠ Cashflow figures are estimates and budgets for the year ahead. Property income and vehicle repayments populate automatically from those tabs — do not enter them again here or they will be double counted.

Adding income

Add your wage, salary, or any other income that does not come from a property or vehicle. Enter the amount and frequency. Rental income from properties pulls in automatically — do not add it again.

Property profit & loss

Each property's rental income minus its expenses shows as either a profit or a loss in Cashflow. If a property costs more to run than it earns it shows as a loss — this is pulled in automatically from the Properties tab.

auto-populated from properties

Vehicle repayments

Vehicle loan repayments appear here automatically from the Vehicles tab. You do not need to enter them manually — they are already accounted for.

auto-populated from vehicles

Adding expenses

Add any other regular living expenses — groceries, utilities, subscriptions, school fees, entertainment. Enter an estimated annual or monthly amount for each.

Net profit or loss

After all income and expenses MangoDesk shows your estimated net profit or loss for the year. This tells you what your financial year will look like before it happens.

Updating through the year

Update figures as your situation changes — pay rises, new expenses, property changes. The annual picture updates instantly so you always have a current view.

Data that auto-populates into Cashflow from other tabs

Rental income→Pulled from Properties automatically
Property expenses→Pulled from Properties automatically
Vehicle repayments→Pulled from Vehicles automatically

Bills — click to expand setup instructions
▼

A bill reminder hub. Enter your bills here so you get alerts on the Dashboard before they are due. Bills do not connect to Cashflow or affect your net worth — this tab is purely for reminders.

⚠ Bills entered here are for reminder purposes only and do not flow into Cashflow or net worth calculations. If you want bills to affect your annual financial picture, add them as expenses in the Cashflow tab.

Adding a bill

Click + Add Bill. Enter the bill name, provider, amount, how often it is due (weekly, monthly, quarterly, annually) and the next due date.

Dashboard alerts

Bills due soon automatically appear as alerts on your Dashboard home screen. You will always know what is coming up before you get caught out.

feeds dashboard alerts

What to add

Add electricity, gas, water, rates, phone, internet, streaming services, gym memberships — anything that arrives periodically and needs to be paid.

Marking as paid

When you pay a bill, mark it as paid and update the next due date. This clears the Dashboard alert and keeps your reminder list accurate going forward.

Creditors — click to expand setup instructions
▼

Track all money you owe — credit cards, personal loans, buy now pay later, money owed to family or friends. The total balance is deducted from your net worth so your financial position reflects what you actually owe.

⚠ If you do not enter your debts here your net worth will appear higher than it really is. Adding creditors gives you an honest picture of your true financial position.

Adding a creditor

Click + Add Creditor. Enter who you owe, the current balance, interest rate, minimum monthly repayment and due date. Add every debt you hold.

Affects your net worth

The total of all creditor balances is automatically deducted from your net worth on the Dashboard. This gives you an honest picture of your real financial position.

feeds dashboard

What to include

Credit cards, personal loans, car loans not in Vehicles, buy now pay later balances (Afterpay, Zip), money owed to family or friends — any debt you are responsible for.

Paying off debt

Update the balance as you pay it down. When a debt is fully cleared, mark it as paid off or delete the entry. Your net worth will improve automatically as balances reduce.

Data that flows from Creditors to other tabs

Total debt balance→Deducted from Net Worth on Dashboard automatically

52 Week Planner — click to expand setup instructions
▼

A weekly budget tool — different to the Cashflow annual view. Enter actual bills and payments week by week and change figures as you go. Great for tracking real spending and staying on top of your budget throughout the year.

⚠ The 52 Week Planner is a standalone budget tool and does not connect to Cashflow or any other tab. Use it alongside Cashflow — Cashflow for your annual estimate, the Planner for your week by week actuals.

How to use it

Each week has a row where you can enter payments, bills and savings for that week. Update it as bills arrive and payments are made — change figures any time.

Ticking off weeks

Tick off each week as you complete it to track your progress through the year. A great way to stay motivated and see how far through your annual budget you are.

Savings challenge

Perfect for the popular 52 week savings challenge — saving an increasing amount each week. Set your target for each week and watch your savings build over the year.

Planning ahead

Use it to plan for big upcoming expenses — car registration, rates, school fees, holidays. Spread them across the weeks so you can see what is coming and budget accordingly.

Add New Account
Each account gets its own income & expense rows in the weekly planner, and shows on your dashboard.

Notepad — click to expand setup instructions
▼

A private scratchpad built into MangoDesk. Jot down financial notes, goals, reminders, or anything you want to keep handy. Nothing here connects to other tabs or affects any calculations.

How to use it

Just click into the notepad and start typing. It saves automatically as you type — there is no save button needed. Your notes are stored privately with your data.

What to use it for

Financial goals, things to follow up, reminders to call your bank or broker, notes from meetings with your accountant, ideas for paying down debt faster. Anything useful.

Private & standalone

Your notes are private and saved with your MangoDesk data. Nothing in the Notepad connects to or affects any other tab or calculation in the dashboard.

standalone
NOTES & TASKS
Notepad

Remaining: 0
Loan Calculator — click to expand setup instructions
▼

A standalone loan calculator — use it to work out repayments before committing to any loan or mortgage. Results do not feed anywhere else in MangoDesk, it is purely a calculation tool.

⚠ The Loan Calculator is a standalone tool only. Results do not flow to Cashflow or any other tab. If you want loan repayments to appear in your annual picture, add them manually in Cashflow.

How to use it

Enter the loan amount, interest rate and loan term. MangoDesk instantly calculates your monthly repayment and shows a full breakdown of principal versus interest over the life of the loan.

Comparing scenarios

Change the loan amount, rate or term to compare different scenarios. See how a higher repayment cuts years off the loan, or how a rate change affects what you pay each month.

Extra repayments

Try increasing your repayment amount to see how many years you can cut off the loan and how much interest you save. Even small extra payments make a significant difference over time.

When to use it

Before taking out a new mortgage, car loan or personal loan. Use it to make sure repayments are affordable before you commit — then add the repayment to Cashflow if you proceed.
